    when(repository.get()).thenReturn("mock foo");
  }


  @Test
  void mockBeanFromContextTest() {

    FooRepository componentFromContext = ac.getBean("fooRepository", FooRepository.class);

    assertThat(componentFromContext.get()).isEqualTo("mock foo");
    verify(repository).get();
  }

}

@Configuration
public class AppConfig {

  ...

  @Bean
  public FooRepository foo2() {
    return new FooRepository();
  }
}

Mocking 하려는 클래스가 bean으로 등록되지 않은 경우, 알아서 등록을 해준다.

@SpringBootTest
class UnitTestApplicationTests {

  @MockBean
  SomeComponent someComponent;

  @Test
  void someComponentTest() {
    when(someComponent.doSomething()).thenReturn("world");

    assertThat(someComponent.doSomething()).isEqualTo("world");
  }
}

public class SomeComponent {
  
  public String  doSomething() {
    return "hello";
  }
}

@Import

컴포넌트 클래스를 등록하기 위해 사용한다. @WebMvcTest, @DataJpaTest 등과 같이 컴포넌트 클래스 등록을 지원하지 않는 어노테이션과 조합해서 사용한다.

굳이 그럴 일은 없겠지만 @SpringBootTest와 같이 사용하면 안된다.@Import의 우선 순위가 더 높기 때문에 먼저 bean으로 등록되고, 그 이후 @SpringBootTest를 통해 로드된 컴포넌트 클래스가 bean으로 등록된다. 따라서 @Import에 지정한 컴포넌트 클래스가 무시될 수 있다.

이해를 돕기 위해 아래 예제를 보자. foo = foo test를 기대하며 bean 오버라이드를 했지만 실제 결과는 foo = foo이다.

@SpringBootTest( properties = "spring.main.allow-bean-definition-overriding=true")
@Import({TestConfig.class})
class UnitTestApplication2Tests {

  @Autowired
  private String foo;

  @Autowired
  private Boolean bar;

  @Test
  void someTest() {
    assertThat(foo).isEqualTo("foo");
    assertThat(bar).isTrue();
  }
}

@Configuration
public class AppConfig {

  @Bean
  public String foo() {
    return "foo";
  }

  @Bean
  public Boolean bar() {
    return true;
  }
}

@TestConfiguration
public class TestConfig {

  @Bean
  public String foo() {
    return "foo test";
  }

}

다음 글에서는 @WebMvcTest, MockMvc, TestRestTemplate 대해서 정리를 해보겠다.

Reference

  • Spring boot reference
  • Spring Framework reference - @ContextConfiguration
  • Spring Framework reference - Context Configuration with Component Classes
  • Spring guides - Testing the Web Layer

item.title: I went to my friend's factory for the first time.
item.description: On Friday evening, July 7, a Friend A, who is a developer at a large entertainment agency, contacted me to have lunch the next day. I really want t...
item.content:encoded:
On Friday evening, July 7, a Friend A, who is a developer at a large entertainment agency, contacted me to have lunch the next day. I really want to go out when I'm at home, but I was wondering what to do because I got lazy when I had to go out. As soon as I thought about it, I immediately answered sushi to his message about what I wanted to eat.

The next day at lunch, a friend drove his car to pick me up 30 minutes earlier than scheduled. When I asked if he bought it because it was a car he hadn't seen before, he said he inherited the car that his father-in-law was riding. Married friends always told me to enjoy my solo life, but it was a moment when I suddenly wanted to get married.

I talked about all sorts of things while eating sushi that my Friend A bought me. Then naturally, the topic of conversation shifted about how other friends were doing, and then we talked about Friend B, who runs a factory in the suburbs outside of Seoul.

Friend A said let's go meet to Friend B because he drove out anyway, and I contacted Friend B right away. He said it would be difficult to see him for a long time because it was his father's birthday, but he thought about whether to go or not because he said it would be good if we came to meet. Still, I thought I wouldn't be able to see you for a long time unless it was this time, so I said I would go back after seeing you briefly, and I left for my friend's factory.

We arrived at the factory after an hour's drive, and Friend B's father greeted us on behalf of Friend B, who went to the client for a while. We went empty-handed because we didn't know we would see him, but I thought it was polite to give him a gift, so I hurriedly went out to the city to buy a cake and give it to him as a gift, and he thanked me and invited me to dinner.

We met with Friend B who returned from the client and talked about the recent situation with each other, and especially talked a lot about how the factory is operated. It was just amazing that my friend, who played with me immaturely as a child, became the president and ordered his subordinates to work. Also, I could feel that he had truly become the president, from his expression of skillfully handling the machine and seriously explaining the technology.

It was fun to talk to Friend B, but We put off the unfinished story and followed him to the dinner table because we shouldn't delay the dinner.

The dinner was attended by the parents of Friend B, his sister's couple, and two nephews. I sat down and exchanged glances with Friend A and regretted, 'I should have just refused and had a quick cup of coffee with Friend B'.

The meal time has been quite long because you made so much food that there is not enough space to put even after attaching two tables. Therefore, we talked a lot for a long time, and contrary to our concerns, we had a very fun time and even told us to sleep a day, so I sincerely agonized. However, we returned to Seoul before it was too late because Friend A promised to pick up his wife.

On my way back. As it started to get dark and cool, I felt emotional, so I came back talking about the old days with my Friend A. It was fun and nice to think of when I was young, but on the other hand, I felt strange when I realized that I was getting older.

item.title: 친구의 공장에 처음으로 가봤다.
item.description: 7월 7일 금요일 저녁, 대형 연예 기획사에서 개발자를 하고 있는 친구A에게서 다음날 점심 식사를 하자고 연락이 왔다. 집에 있으면 엄청 나가고 싶은데, 희안하게 막상 외출할 일이 생기니 귀찮아져서 어떻게 할지 고민하고 있었다. 그러던 중, 뭐먹고 싶냐는 메시지에...
item.content:encoded:
7월 7일 금요일 저녁, 대형 연예 기획사에서 개발자를 하고 있는 친구A에게서 다음날 점심 식사를 하자고 연락이 왔다. 집에 있으면 엄청 나가고 싶은데, 희안하게 막상 외출할 일이 생기니 귀찮아져서 어떻게 할지 고민하고 있었다. 그러던 중, 뭐먹고 싶냐는 메시지에 초밥이라고 냉큼 대답해 버렸다.

다음날 점심, 친구가 예정보다 30분 일찍 자가용을 몰고 데리러왔다. 못보던 차라서 샀냐 물었더니, 장인께서 타시던 차를 받았다고 했다. 결혼한 친구들은 항상 솔로 생활을 즐기라고 하던데, 문득 결혼이 하고 싶어지는 순간이었다.

친구A가 사준 초밥을 먹으며 정말 별의별 얘기를 다 했다. 그러다 자연스럽게 다른 친구들은 뭐하고 지내는지에 대해서 화제가 옮걌는데, 지금은 서울을 벗어나 근교에서 공장을 운영하고 있는 친구B 이야기가 나왔다.

친구A는 차를 몰고 나온 김에 친구B를 보러 가보자 말했고 나는 곧바로 그 친구B에게 연락을 해봤다. 아버지 생신이라 오래 보기는 힘들것 같지만 와도 좋다고 말해서, 갈지말지 잠깐 고민했다. 그래도 이럴 때 아니면 한참 동안 못볼 것 같아서 짧게 얼굴만 보고 돌아 가겠다고 하고 친구의 공장으로 출발했다.

1시간 정도 달려서 공장에 도착했고 잠시 거래처에 나간 친구B를 대신해 친구B의 아버지께서 우리를 맞아주셨다. 뵐 줄 몰랐기 때문에 빈손으로 갔는데, 뭔가 선물을 드리는게 도리인 것 같아서 부랴부랴 시내로 나가 케이크를 사서 선물로 드렸더니 저녁 식사에 초대해 주셨다.

거래처에서 돌아온 친구B와 만나 서로 근황에 대해서 이야기 했고, 특히 공장 운영은 어떤지 이야기를 많이 나눴다. 어릴적 철없이 함께 놀던 친구가, 어느새 사장이 되서 직원을 부리는 것이 마냥 신기했고, 언제 기술을 익혔는지 기계를 능숙하게 다루고 기술을 얘기할 때 그의 표정에서 진정 사장이 됐음을 느낄 수 있었다.

친구B와 이야기를 하는 건 즐거웠지만, 우리들로 인해서 저녁 식사가 늦어지면 안되기 때문에 못다한 이야기는 뒤로 미루고 친구B를 따라 식사 자리에 갔다.

그 자리에는 친구B의 부모님, 누나네 부부와 두명의 조카까지 모두 모여있었다. 자리를 잡고 앉아 친구A와 눈빛을 주고 받으며, '그냥 거절하고 친구B와 간단히 커피나 한잔하고 돌아갈껄' 하고 후회했다.

밥상 2개를 붙이고도 놓을 공간이 부족할 정도로 음식을 많이 만드셔서, 식사 시간이 꽤 길어졌다. 따라서 긴 시간 많은 얘기를 나눴는데, 우려와는 반대로 굉장히 재밌게 시간을 보냈고 하루 자고 가라고까지 말씀하셔서 진심으로 고민했다. 하지만 친구A가 아내를 데리러 가기로 약속을 했기 때문에 시간이 더 늦어지기 전에 서울로 돌아오게 되었다.

돌아가는 길. 날이 어두워지기 시작하고 선선해지니 감성적인 기분이 들어서 친구A와 옛날 이야기를 잔뜩 하면서 돌아왔다. 어릴적 생각이 나서 즐겁고 좋았지만, 한편으로는 나이를 먹어간다는 걸 실감하게 돼서 오묘한 기분이 드는 귀가길 이었다.

item.title: Create Mocks with Mockito in Unit Test
item.description: Mockito is a Mocking framework that is used to create Mock objects to control situations that developers cannot control. However, if it is possible...
item.content:encoded:
💡 If you are not familiar with the terminology for test double, read Let's find out about Spies, Stubs, Fakes, and Mocks.

Mockito is a Mocking framework that is used to create Mock objects to control situations that developers cannot control. However, if it is possible not to use the Mock object through efforts such as changing the code design, doing so can help write good code.

Create Mocks

Mockito.mock() allows you to create a Mock object, and returns the default value for each type if you do not make any settings. The default values are summarized as follows.

  • if the return value is void, nothing happens.
  • If the return value is of the Primitive type, it returns the default value of the primitive type.
  • if the return value is of the Object type, Returns null.
  • If the return value is of the Optional type, Returns Optional.empty().
  • If the return value is of the Collection type, Returns empty collection.
class MockBasicTest {
  @Test
  void createMockTest() {
    var mock = Mockito.mock(DefaultValueTest.class);

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

class DefaultValueTest {
  public void voidMethod() {

  }

  public int primitiveReturn() {
    return 10;
  }

  public String objectReturn() {
    return "string";
  }

  public Optional<Double> optionalReturn() {
    return Optional.of(3.14159);
  }

  public List<Character> collectionReturn() {
    return List.of('1','2');
  }

}

If the way of using the annotation is convenient, it can be used.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {
  
  @Test
  void createMockTest(@Mock DefaultValueTest mock) {

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

To share between tests, the Mocks may be declared in the field to minimize redundancy.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;
  
  @Test
  void createMockTest() {

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

Create Spies

Spies can also be created in a similar way to Mocks. As mentioned in the article related to the test double, it acts like a real object if there is no setting.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Test
  void createSpyTest() {
    DefaultValueTest spy = Mockito.spy(DefaultValueTest.class);

    assertDoesNotThrow(spy::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(10, spy.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(List.of('1','2'), spy.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.of(3.14159), spy.getOptionalValue());
    assertNotNull(spy.getObjectValue());
  }
}

If you use the annotation, you can only declare on the field.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Spy
  DefaultValueTest spy;
  
  @Test
  void createSpyTest() {
    assertDoesNotThrow(spy::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(10, spy.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(List.of('1','2'), spy.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.of(3.14159), spy.getOptionalValue());
    assertNotNull(spy.getObjectValue());
  }
}

Stubbing

Let's find out how to stub so that the Mocks can return the specified value.

Without argument

Stubbing can be done by combining when(), which specifies conditions, and thenXX(), which specifies actions.

💡 Since the example used in the Mocks and Stub creation chapter is used as it is, the overlapping parts will be excluded.

  • thenReturn() : Literal values can be specified according to the return type of method that enters when clause.
@Test
void thenReturnTest() {
  Mockito.when(mock.getObjectValue()).thenReturn("Hello world");

  assertEquals(mock.getObjectValue(), "Hello world");
}
  • thenThrow() : Running a method that meets the conditions returns an exception.
@Test
void thenThrowTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getOptionalValue()).thenThrow(new IllegalStateException());

  assertThrows(IllegalStateException.class, mock::getOptionalValue);
}
  • then() & thenAnswer() : When a method meets the conditions, it executes a callback.
@Test
void thenAnswerTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getCollectionValue()).thenAnswer((invocation)->{
    Object[] args = invocation.getArguments();
    Object mock = invocation.getMock();
    System.out.println("called with arguments: " + Arrays.toString(args));
    return List.of('t','e','s','t');
  });

  assertEquals(mock.getCollectionValue(), List.of('t','e','s','t'));
}
  • thenCallRealMethod() : Running a method that meets the conditions returns the execution value of the actual method.
@Test
void thenCallRealMethodTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getCollectionValue()).thenCallRealMethod();

  assertEquals(mock.getCollectionValue(), List.of('1','2'));
}

With argument

The method of stubbing is the same as when arguments are not included, but there is a difference in that only the specified literal value argument or argument type is stubbed.

  • When using literal values
    Only the corresponding literal value argument is stubbed, and using other arguments returns the default value as mentioned above.
@Test
void stubWithLiteralArgumentTest() {
  List mock = Mockito.mock(List.class);
  when(mock.get(0)).thenReturn(10);

  assertEquals(10, mock.get(0));
  assertNull(mock.get(1));
}
  • When using ArgumentMatchers
    You can specify the type to hand over to the argument. Each matchers names are intuitive, so no explanation is required, so only examples of use are attached.
...
import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.*;
import static org.mockito.Mockito.when;


@Test
void stubWithArgumentMatchersTest() {
  Map mock = Mockito.mock(Map.class);

  when(mock.get(isNull())).thenReturn(0);
  when(mock.get(anyBoolean())).thenReturn(1);
  when(mock.get(anyInt())).thenReturn(10);
  when(mock.get(anyDouble())).thenReturn(20);
  when(mock.get(anyString())).thenReturn(30);
  when(mock.get(anyMap())).thenReturn(100);
  when(mock.get(anyCollection())).thenReturn(200);
  when(mock.get(any(Character[].class))).thenReturn(300);
  when(mock.get(any(DefaultValueTest.class))).thenReturn(1000);

  assertEquals(0, mock.get(null));
  assertEquals(1, mock.get(false));
  assertEquals(10, mock.get(1234));
  assertEquals(20, mock.get(3.14));
  assertEquals(30, mock.get("string"));
  assertEquals(100, mock.get(Map.of()));
  assertEquals(200, mock.get(List.of()));
  assertEquals(300, mock.get(new Character[]{'A'}));
  assertEquals(1000, mock.get(new DefaultValueTest()));
}

@Test
void stubWithAnyArgumentTest() {
  Map mock = Mockito.mock(Map.class);

  when(mock.get(any())).thenReturn(0);

  assertEquals(0, mock.get(null));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(false));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(1234));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(3.14));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get("string"));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(Map.of()));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(List.of()));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(new Character[]{'A'}));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(new DefaultValueTest()));
}
  • When using literal values and ArgumentMatters together.
    When Matchers and literal values are applied together, Matchers have priority.
  @Test
  void stubWithArgumentTest() {
    List mock = Mockito.mock(List.class);
    lenient().when(mock.get(0)).thenReturn(10);
    lenient().when(mock.get(1)).thenReturn(100);
    when(mock.get(anyInt())).thenReturn(1000);

    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(0));
    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(1));
    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(2));
  }

💡 lenient() is a setting that applies a loose rule, and does not cause an error even if a specified stub is not used.

In addition to the above-mentioned matchers, there are various matchers. If you want to know about this, please refer to ArgumentMatchers javadoc.

If return type is void

Only stubbing that causes an exception is possible. This is because the return value can be stubbed, but a compilation error occurs because the method return type is void.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;

  @Test
  void stubVoidThrowRuntimeExceptionTest() {
    Mockito.doThrow(new IllegalArgumentException()).when(mock).invokeVoidMethod();

    assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> mock.invokeVoidMethod());
  }
}

Throws must be declared in the method specified to cause a Checked Exception.

class DefaultValueTest {
  public void invokeVoidMethodThrow() throws IllegalAccessException { }

  ...
}

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;
  
  @Test
  void stubVoidThrowCheckedExceptionTest() throws IllegalAccessException {
    Mockito.doThrow(new IllegalAccessException()).when(mock).invokeVoidMethodThrow();

    assertThrows(IllegalAccessException.class, () -> mock.invokeVoidMethodThrow());
  }
  ...
}

Consecutive calls

Let's find out how to stub when calling the same method with the same argument.

  • Using varargs
    If the same operation is performed and the literal value is different, stubbing can be performed using a varargs.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithVarargTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(0, 10, 200);

  assertEquals(0,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(10,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(200,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}
  • Using method chaining
    When stubbing multiple actions, perform a method chain.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithChainingTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue())
    .thenReturn(0, 5)
    .thenThrow(new IllegalArgumentException())
    .thenCallRealMethod();

  assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(5, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(10, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}
  • ⛔️ Cautions
    if instead of chaining thenReturn() calls, multiple stubbing with the same matchers or arguments is used, then each stubbing will override the previous one.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithMultipleStubTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(0);
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(10);
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(20);

  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}

thenXXX() vs doXXX()

The stubbing methods examined above, thenReturn(), thenThrow(), thenAnswer(), and thenCallRealMethod(), have methods that start with do, such as doReturn(). After that, we will call them thenXXX() and doXXX().

Since doXXX() passes the Object type as an argument, the type safety is poor because it checks the type at runtime. For this reason, it is recommended to use thenXXX() if possible. So when was doXXX() made for use?

  • When stubbing the void return type
    Actually, I used doThrow() when I stubbed the void in the Just the previous chapter. Methods whose return type is void use doXXX() because using thenXXX() results in a compilation error.
  • When Using spies
    It is used to prevent side effects as the actual function is called when stubbing.
@Spy
ArrayList spy;

@Test
void spyCauseIndexOutOfBoundsException() {
  when(spy.get(0)).thenReturn("foo");    // IndexOutOfBoundsException

  assertEquals("foo", spy.get(0));
}

@Test
void spyReturnString() {
  doReturn("foo").when(spy).get(0);
  
  assertEquals("foo", spy.get(0));
}

Verification

To verify that the specified method has been executed, verify() is used in combination with VerificationMode.

  • times(): Check how many times the specified method has been invoked. If the argument is not passed, use the default values of times(1).
@Test
void verifyTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}
  • atLeastOnce(),atMostOnce(): Check the method is invoked at least/most once.
  • atLeast(), atMost(): Check that at least/most the specified number of invokes has been made.
  • never(): Verify that the specified method is not invoked.
@Test
void verifyWithNeverTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  verify(mock, never()).invokeVoidMethod();
}
  • verifyNoInteractions(): Check that the specified Mock object has not interacted with anything.
@Test
void verifyWithVerifyNoInteractionsTest() {
  ArrayList arrayListMock = mock(ArrayList.class);
  arrayListMock.get(0);
  arrayListMock.get(1);

  HashMap hashMapMock = mock(HashMap.class);
  verifyNoInteractions(hashMapMock);
}
  • inOrder(): Check that methods are invoked in order.
@Test
void verifyInOrderTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getOptionalValue();

  InOrder inOrder = inOrder(mock);
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getOptionalValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}

💡 Even if the same method is called, it must be checked in order.

@Test
void verifyInOrderTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getOptionalValue();
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  InOrder inOrder = inOrder(mock);
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(2)).getPrimitiveValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getOptionalValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}

If you need more information about Verification mode, please refer to Verification mode javadoc.

@InjectMocks

Annotation that makes you inject both Mock and Spy objects.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MemberTest {

  @Mock
  MemberRepository mockMemberRepository;

  @Spy
  Notification spyNotification;

  @InjectMocks
  MemberService service;

  @Test
  void registerMemberTest() {
    // Given
    Member member = generateMember();
    when(mockMemberRepository.findByEmail("test@test.com")).thenReturn(Optional.empty());
    when(mockMemberRepository.save(any(Member.class))).thenReturn(member);

    // When
    service.registerMember("test@test.com", "password");

    // Then
    verify(mockNotification).trigger(any(Member.class));
  }

  @Test
  void registerMemberThrowTest() {
    // Given
    Member member = generateMember();
    when(mockMemberRepository.findByEmail("test@test.com")).thenReturn(Optional.of(member));

    // When
    IllegalArgumentException exception = assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> service.registerMember("test@test.com", "password"));

    // Then
    assertEquals("This member is already registered.", exception.getMessage());
  }

  private Member generateMember() {
    return new Member("test@test.com", "password");
  }
}

public class MemberService {
  private final MemberRepository repository;
  private final Notification notification;

  public MemberService(MemberRepository repository, Notification notification) {
    this.repository = repository;
    this.notification = notification;
  }

  public Member registerMember(String email, String password) {
    Optional<Member> existMember = repository.findByEmail(email);
    if (existMember.isPresent()) {
      throw new IllegalArgumentException("This member is already registered.");
    }

    Member savedMember = repository.save(new Member(email, password));
    notification.trigger(savedMember);

    return savedMember;
  }
}

public class Member {
  private String email;

  private String password;

  public Member(String email, String password) {
    this.email = email;
    this.password = password;
  }

  public String getPassword() {
    return password;
  }

  public String getEmail() {
    return email;
  }
}

public interface MemberRepository {
  Optional<Member> findByEmail(String email);

  Member save(Member member);
}

public interface Notification {
  void trigger(Member savedMember);
}

Reference

  • Mockito Javadoc

item.title: 단위 테스트에서 Mockito를 사용하여 Mock 객체 만들기
item.description: Mockito는 mocking framework로, 개발자가 제어할 수 없는 상황을 통제하기 위해서 Mock 객체를 생성하는 용도로 사용한다. 다만, 코드 설계를 바꾸는 등의 노력을 통해서 Mock 객체를 사용하지 않을 수 있다면, 그렇게 하는것이 좋은 코드를 작...
item.content:encoded:
💡 테스트 대역에 관한 용어가 익숙치 않다면 Spy, Stub, Fake 그리고 Mock에 대해서 알아보자를 읽어보길 바란다.

Mockito는 mocking framework로, 개발자가 제어할 수 없는 상황을 통제하기 위해서 Mock 객체를 생성하는 용도로 사용한다. 다만, 코드 설계를 바꾸는 등의 노력을 통해서 Mock 객체를 사용하지 않을 수 있다면, 그렇게 하는것이 좋은 코드를 작성하는데 도움이 될 수 있다.

Mock 객체 생성

Mockito.mock()을 사용하면 Mock 객체를 생성할 수 있으며, 별다른 설정을 하지 않는다면 각 타입에 따라 기본 값을 반환한다. 기본값을 정리해보자면 다음과 같다.

  • 리턴 값이 void라면 아무 일도 발생하지 않는다.
  • 리턴 값이 원시 타입이라면 원시 타입 기본값을 반환한다.
  • 리턴 값이 객체 타입이라면 null을 반환한다.
  • 리턴 값이 Optional 타입이라면 Optional.empty()를 반환한다.
  • 리턴 값이 컬렉션 타입이라면 빈 컬렉션을 반환한다.
class MockBasicTest {
  @Test
  void createMockTest() {
    var mock = Mockito.mock(DefaultValueTest.class);

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

class DefaultValueTest {
  public void voidMethod() {

  }

  public int primitiveReturn() {
    return 10;
  }

  public String objectReturn() {
    return "string";
  }

  public Optional<Double> optionalReturn() {
    return Optional.of(3.14159);
  }

  public List<Character> collectionReturn() {
    return List.of('1','2');
  }

}

어노테이션 방식이 편하다면, 이를 이용할 수도 있다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {
  
  @Test
  void createMockTest(@Mock DefaultValueTest mock) {

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

테스트 간 공유하도록 필드에 선언해서 중복을 최소화 할 수도 있다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;
  
  @Test
  void createMockTest() {

    assertDoesNotThrow(mock::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(Collections.emptyList(), mock.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.empty(), mock.getOptionalValue());
    assertNull(mock.getObjectValue());
  }
}

Spy 객체 생성

Mock 객체 생성했던 것과 비슷한 방법으로 Spy 객체도 생성할 수 있다. 테스트 대역 관련 글에서 언급했던 것처럼, 별다른 설정을 하지 않는다면 실제 객체처럼 동작한다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Test
  void createSpyTest() {
    DefaultValueTest spy = Mockito.spy(DefaultValueTest.class);

    assertDoesNotThrow(spy::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(10, spy.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(List.of('1','2'), spy.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.of(3.14159), spy.getOptionalValue());
    assertNotNull(spy.getObjectValue());
  }
}

어노테이션 이용시 필드에만 선언이 가능하다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Spy
  DefaultValueTest spy;
  
  @Test
  void createSpyTest() {
    assertDoesNotThrow(spy::invokeVoidMethod);
    assertEquals(10, spy.getPrimitiveValue());
    assertEquals(List.of('1','2'), spy.getCollectionValue());
    assertEquals(Optional.of(3.14159), spy.getOptionalValue());
    assertNotNull(spy.getObjectValue());
  }
}

Stubbing

Mock 객체가 지정한 값을 반환할 수 있도록 stubbing 하는 방법을 알아보겠다.

Without argument

조건을 지정하는 when()과 동작을 지정하는 thenXX()를 조합해서 stubbing 할 수 있다.

💡 객체 생성 단원에서 사용하던 예제를 그대로 사용하기 때문에 중복되는 부분은 제외하도록 하겠다.

  • thenReturn() : 조건에 들어가는 메소드의 반환 타입에 맞춰 리터럴 값을 지정할 수 있다.
@Test
void thenReturnTest() {
  Mockito.when(mock.getObjectValue()).thenReturn("Hello world");

  assertEquals(mock.getObjectValue(), "Hello world");
}
  • thenThrow() : 조건에 맞는 메소드를 실행하면 예외를 반환한다.
@Test
void thenThrowTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getOptionalValue()).thenThrow(new IllegalStateException());

  assertThrows(IllegalStateException.class, mock::getOptionalValue);
}
  • then() & thenAnswer() : 조건에 맞는 메소드를 실행하면 콜백을 실행한다.
@Test
void thenAnswerTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getCollectionValue()).thenAnswer((invocation)->{
    Object[] args = invocation.getArguments();
    Object mock = invocation.getMock();
    System.out.println("called with arguments: " + Arrays.toString(args));
    return List.of('t','e','s','t');
  });

  assertEquals(mock.getCollectionValue(), List.of('t','e','s','t'));
}
  • thenCallRealMethod() : 조건에 맞는 메소드를 실행하면 실제 메소드의 실행값을 반환한다.
@Test
void thenCallRealMethodTest(){
  Mockito.when(mock.getCollectionValue()).thenCallRealMethod();

  assertEquals(mock.getCollectionValue(), List.of('1','2'));
}

With argument

인수를 포함하지 않을 때와 stubbing 하는 방법은 동일하나, 지정한 리터럴 값 인수나 인수 타입만 stubbing 되는 차이가 있다.

  • 리터럴 값 사용시
    해당 리터럴 값 인수만 stubbing 되며, 다른 인수를 이용하면 위에서 언급했던 것처럼 기본값을 반환한다.
@Test
void stubWithLiteralArgumentTest() {
  List mock = Mockito.mock(List.class);
  when(mock.get(0)).thenReturn(10);

  assertEquals(10, mock.get(0));
  assertNull(mock.get(1));
}
  • ArgumentMatchers 사용시
    인수로 넘길 타입을 지정할 수 있다. Matchers 이름이 직관적이라서 굳이 설명이 필요하지 않기 때문에 사용 예제만 첨부한다.
...
import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.*;
import static org.mockito.Mockito.when;


@Test
void stubWithArgumentMatchersTest() {
  Map mock = Mockito.mock(Map.class);

  when(mock.get(isNull())).thenReturn(0);
  when(mock.get(anyBoolean())).thenReturn(1);
  when(mock.get(anyInt())).thenReturn(10);
  when(mock.get(anyDouble())).thenReturn(20);
  when(mock.get(anyString())).thenReturn(30);
  when(mock.get(anyMap())).thenReturn(100);
  when(mock.get(anyCollection())).thenReturn(200);
  when(mock.get(any(Character[].class))).thenReturn(300);
  when(mock.get(any(DefaultValueTest.class))).thenReturn(1000);

  assertEquals(0, mock.get(null));
  assertEquals(1, mock.get(false));
  assertEquals(10, mock.get(1234));
  assertEquals(20, mock.get(3.14));
  assertEquals(30, mock.get("string"));
  assertEquals(100, mock.get(Map.of()));
  assertEquals(200, mock.get(List.of()));
  assertEquals(300, mock.get(new Character[]{'A'}));
  assertEquals(1000, mock.get(new DefaultValueTest()));
}

@Test
void stubWithAnyArgumentTest() {
  Map mock = Mockito.mock(Map.class);

  when(mock.get(any())).thenReturn(0);

  assertEquals(0, mock.get(null));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(false));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(1234));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(3.14));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get("string"));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(Map.of()));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(List.of()));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(new Character[]{'A'}));
  assertEquals(0, mock.get(new DefaultValueTest()));
}
  • 리터럴 값과 ArgumentMatchers를 같이 사용시
    Matchers와 리터럴 값을 같이 적용하면 matchers가 우선순위를 가진다.
  @Test
  void stubWithArgumentTest() {
    List mock = Mockito.mock(List.class);
    lenient().when(mock.get(0)).thenReturn(10);
    lenient().when(mock.get(1)).thenReturn(100);
    when(mock.get(anyInt())).thenReturn(1000);

    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(0));
    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(1));
    assertEquals(1000, mock.get(2));
  }

💡 lenient()는 느슨한 규칙을 적용하는 설정으로, 지정된 Stub이 사용되지 않아도 에러를 발생시키지 않는다.

위에서 언급한 matchers 외에도 다양한 matchers가 있다. 이에 대해 알고 싶다면 ArgumentMatchers javadoc을 참고하길 바란다.

반환 타입이 void인 경우

예외를 발생시키는 stubbing만 가능하다. 반환 값을 stubbing 가능하지만 메소드 반환 타입이 void이기 때문에 컴파일 에러가 발생하기 때문이다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;

  @Test
  void stubVoidThrowRuntimeExceptionTest() {
    Mockito.doThrow(new IllegalArgumentException()).when(mock).invokeVoidMethod();

    assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> mock.invokeVoidMethod());
  }
}

Checked Exception을 발생시키려면 지정한 메소드에 throws가 선언되어 있어야한다.

class DefaultValueTest {
  public void invokeVoidMethodThrow() throws IllegalAccessException { }

  ...
}

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MockitoBasicTest {

  @Mock
  DefaultValueTest mock;
  
  @Test
  void stubVoidThrowCheckedExceptionTest() throws IllegalAccessException {
    Mockito.doThrow(new IllegalAccessException()).when(mock).invokeVoidMethodThrow();

    assertThrows(IllegalAccessException.class, () -> mock.invokeVoidMethodThrow());
  }
  ...
}

Consecutive calls

동일 메소드를 같은 인수로 호출할 때 stubbing 하는 방법을 알아보자.

  • 가변인자 사용
    같은 동작을 하고 리터럴 값만 다르다면 가변인자를 사용해서 stubbing 할 수 있다.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithVarargTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(0, 10, 200);

  assertEquals(0,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(10,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(200,mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}
  • 체이닝 사용
    여러 동작을 stubbing 할 때에는 체이닝을 하면 된다.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithChainingTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue())
    .thenReturn(0, 5)
    .thenThrow(new IllegalArgumentException())
    .thenCallRealMethod();

  assertEquals(0, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(5, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(10, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}
  • ⛔️ 주의
    동일한 matchers 또는 인수를 가진 메소드를 여러번 stubbing 하는 경우, 각 stubbing은 이전 stubbing을 덮어쓴다.
@Test
void ConsecutiveCallsWithMultipleStubTest() {
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(0);
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(10);
  when(mock.getPrimitiveValue()).thenReturn(20);

  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
  assertEquals(20, mock.getPrimitiveValue());
}

thenXXX() vs doXXX()

위에서 살펴봤던 stubbing 메소드 thenReturn(), thenThrow(), thenAnswer() 그리고 thenCallRealMethod()는 doReturn()처럼 do로 시작하는 이름의 메소드가 있다. 이후 thenXXX()와 doXXX()라고 부르도록 하겠다.

doXXX()는 인수로 Object 타입을 넘기므로 런타임에 타입 체크를 하기 때문에 타입 안전성이 떨어진다. 이 때문에 가능하면 thenXXX()를 사용하는 것이 좋다. 그렇다면 doXXX()는 언제 사용하기 위해 만들어졌을까?

  • void 리턴 타입을 stubbing 할 때
    사실 바로 윗 단원에서 void를 stubbing 할때 doThrow()를 사용했었다. 반환 타입이 void인 메소드는 thenXXX()를 사용하면 컴파일 에러가 발생하므로 doXXX()를 사용한다.
  • Spy 객체 사용시
    Stubbing 할 때 실제 기능이 호출되면서 예상치 못한 동작, 즉 사이드 이펙트가 발생하기 때문에 이를 예방하기 위해 사용한다.
@Spy
ArrayList spy;

@Test
void spyCauseIndexOutOfBoundsException() {
  when(spy.get(0)).thenReturn("foo");    // IndexOutOfBoundsException

  assertEquals("foo", spy.get(0));
}

@Test
void spyReturnString() {
  doReturn("foo").when(spy).get(0);
  
  assertEquals("foo", spy.get(0));
}

검증하기

지정한 메소드가 실행 됐는지 확인하기 위해 verify()를 사용하며, VerificationMode와 조합해서 사용한다.

  • times(): 지정한 메소드가 몇 번 호출됐는지 확인한다. 인자를 넘기지 않으면 기본값인 times(1)을 사용한다.
@Test
void verifyTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}
  • atLeastOnce(),atMostOnce(): 적어도/최대 한 번 호출됐는지 확인한다.
  • atLeast(), atMost(): 최소/최대로 지정한 수만큼만 호출됐는지 확인한다.
  • never(): 지정한 메소드가 호출되지 않았는지 확인한다.
@Test
void verifyWithNeverTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  verify(mock, never()).invokeVoidMethod();
}
  • verifyNoInteractions(): 지정한 Mock 객체가 어떠한 상호작용도 하지 않았는지 확인한다.
@Test
void verifyWithVerifyNoInteractionsTest() {
  ArrayList arrayListMock = mock(ArrayList.class);
  arrayListMock.get(0);
  arrayListMock.get(1);

  HashMap hashMapMock = mock(HashMap.class);
  verifyNoInteractions(hashMapMock);
}
  • inOrder(): 순서대로 호출 되었는지 확인한다.
@Test
void verifyInOrderTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getOptionalValue();

  InOrder inOrder = inOrder(mock);
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getOptionalValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}

💡 같은 메소드를 호출했더라도, 반드시 순서에 맞춰서 확인해야 한다.

@Test
void verifyInOrderTest() {
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();
  mock.getOptionalValue();
  mock.getPrimitiveValue();

  InOrder inOrder = inOrder(mock);
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(2)).getPrimitiveValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getOptionalValue();
  inOrder.verify(mock, times(1)).getPrimitiveValue();
}

Verification mode에 대해 정보가 더 필요하다면 Verification mode javadoc을 참고하길 바란다.

@InjectMocks

Mock 객체와 spy 객체를 모두 주입하도록 만드는 어노테이션이다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class MemberTest {

  @Mock
  MemberRepository mockMemberRepository;

  @Spy
  Notification spyNotification;

  @InjectMocks
  MemberService service;

  @Test
  void registerMemberTest() {
    // Given
    Member member = generateMember();
    when(mockMemberRepository.findByEmail("test@test.com")).thenReturn(Optional.empty());
    when(mockMemberRepository.save(any(Member.class))).thenReturn(member);

    // When
    service.registerMember("test@test.com", "password");

    // Then
    verify(mockNotification).trigger(any(Member.class));
  }

  @Test
  void registerMemberThrowTest() {
    // Given
    Member member = generateMember();
    when(mockMemberRepository.findByEmail("test@test.com")).thenReturn(Optional.of(member));

    // When
    IllegalArgumentException exception = assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> service.registerMember("test@test.com", "password"));

    // Then
    assertEquals("This member is already registered.", exception.getMessage());
  }

  private Member generateMember() {
    return new Member("test@test.com", "password");
  }
}

public class MemberService {
  private final MemberRepository repository;
  private final Notification notification;

  public MemberService(MemberRepository repository, Notification notification) {
    this.repository = repository;
    this.notification = notification;
  }

  public Member registerMember(String email, String password) {
    Optional<Member> existMember = repository.findByEmail(email);
    if (existMember.isPresent()) {
      throw new IllegalArgumentException("This member is already registered.");
    }

    Member savedMember = repository.save(new Member(email, password));
    notification.trigger(savedMember);

    return savedMember;
  }
}

public class Member {
  private String email;

  private String password;

  public Member(String email, String password) {
    this.email = email;
    this.password = password;
  }

  public String getPassword() {
    return password;
  }

  public String getEmail() {
    return email;
  }
}

public interface MemberRepository {
  Optional<Member> findByEmail(String email);

  Member save(Member member);
}

public interface Notification {
  void trigger(Member savedMember);
}

Reference

  • Mockito Javadoc

item.title: Let's find out about Spies, Stubs, Fakes, and Mocks.
item.description: While searching for materials to learn about Mockito, I found a blogger calls fake object to a stub in an article about Test Double I thought all t...
item.content:encoded:
While searching for materials to learn about Mockito, I found a blogger calls fake object to a stub in an article about Test Double I thought all the fake objects were Mocks, but I was wondering why they used the word Stub, so I looked into them.

Test double

Test double is derived from the stunt double that replaces the actor in the movie, and refers to an object that proceeds on behalf of the object when it is difficult to proceed with the test. In other words, it refers to an object that simplifies the components that SUT relies on so that it can focus only on the test target when testing.

Here, the test target is called SUT by shortening the System Under Test, and the component on which SUT depends is called DOC by shortening the Dependent-On Component.

If the collaboration between SUT and DOC is not awkward, the function (behavior) is viewed as a unit and tested together is called a sociable test. In the case of Sociable test, it is difficult to test situations that SUT cannot control, such as the current time, or code expensive to test such as Email.

Conversely, breaking the dependence of SUT and DOC and testing them on a single module basis is called a solitary test. On the contrary, it is called solitary test to break the dependency of SUT and DOC, and test it in a single module unit. The DOC created at this time is the test band.

💡 To describing the need for the Test Double, the sociable test was expressed somewhat negatively, but in reality, it is not. Later, I will summarize the pros and cons of the Solitary test and Sociable test.

When is it used?

  • When you control an uncontrollable situation.

Random numbers, current time, and reading certain records from DB do not always guarantee the same results. In addition, network errors or external service conditions may not always be normal. In this way, it can be used to control situations that SUT cannot control as consistent situations.

  • When testing cost is expensive.

Templates sent by e-mail, SMS, or messenger, or message contents sent to message queues are difficult to test because of the high cost of testing. In this case, the contents necessary for verification may be stored in the Test Double and then verified.

  • When you build an environment for creating tests.

It can be used if a specific function is not yet implemented and testing is not possible.

Types of Test doubles

Dummy

It is the most basic test double, which is used only to pass over to the parameter value and is not actually used. This includes an object with only a default value filled in the field or an object with an empty method implementation unit.

@Test
void dummyTest() {
    Article dummyArticle = new Article("", "", new Date(), 0);
    
    assertThrows(Exception.class, () -> articleService.register(dummyArticle));
}

class ConsolePrinter {
    public void print(String text){
        System.out.println(text);
    }
}

class DummyConsolePrinter extends ConsolePrinter {
    @Override
    public void print(String text) {}
}

Stub

A step up from dummies, stubs are typically return hard-coded values.

class StubArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    @Override
    public List<Article> getAll() {
        return List.of(
                new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1),
                new Article("title_2", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 8, 23, 10, 50), 0)
        );
    }
}

Spy

The same operation as the actual object may be performed or may be operated as a stub. When called, it stores the data needed for test verification.

class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    void spyTest() {
        SpyArticleRepository spy = new SpyArticleRepository();
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spy);
        Article stub1 = new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1);
        Article stub2 = new Article("title_2", "content_2", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 6, 29, 11, 15), 2);

        Long id1 = service.register(stub1);
        Long id2 = service.register(stub2);

        assertEquals(1, id1);
        assertEquals(2, id2);
        assertEquals(2, spy.getArticles().size());      // Spy's save behavior happened 2 times

    }
}

class SpyArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    private List<Article> articles = new ArrayList<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        articles.add(article);
        return 1L;
    }

    public List<Article> getArticles() {
        return articles;
    }
}

class ArticleService {
    private ArticleRepository repository;

    public ArticleService(ArticleRepository repository) {
        this.repository = repository;
    }

    public Long register(Article article) {
        return repository.save(article);
    }
}

Fake

It is an object implemented by simplifying the operation of an actual object, and although it is not hard-coded and operates dynamically, it is not suitable for use in a production environment. An example is an in-memory DB using HashMap.

class ArticleRepositoryFake implements ArticleRepository {

    private Map<Long, Article> articles = new HashMap<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        Long existId = article.getId();
        if (existId == null) {
            return createNewArticle(article);
        }
        updateOldArticle(existId, article);
        return existId;
    }

    private void updateOldArticle(Long existId, Article article) {
        articles.put(existId, article);
    }

    private Long createNewArticle(Article article) {
        Long id = (long) (getAll().size() + 1);
        this.articles.put(id, article);
        return id;
    }

}

Mock

It is an object created dynamically through the Mock library. Depending on the configuration, it works like Dummy, Stub, or Spy.

💡 When a Spy object is created using a Mock library or testing framework, it usually behaves like a real object and can optionally be used by stubbing the desired method. I've checked on Mockito, Mockk, Spock, and Jest that I'm interested in, but if there's anything else, please let me know.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a dummy")
    void mockTest1(@Mock Member dummyMember) {
        Article article = new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, dummyMember);
        ArticleRepository repository = new ArticleRepository();

        assertDoesNotThrow(() -> repository.save(article));
    }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a stub")
    void mockTest2(@Mock ArticleRepository stubRepository) {
        when(stubRepository.findById(anyLong())).thenReturn(new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, new Member("","")));
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(stubRepository);

        Article article = service.fetch(1L);

        assertEquals("Title 1", article.getTitle());

    }

    @Spy
    ArticleRepository spyRepository;

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a spy")
    void mockTest3() {
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spyRepository);

        service.fetch(1L);
        service.fetch(2L);

        verify(spyRepository, times(2)).findById(anyLong());

    }
}

Limit of test band

  • It has low resistance to refactoring.

Low resistance to refactoring is in line with increasing code management costs. Let's look at the example. When using the Test double, the internal implementation of the SUT is externally exposed as follows.

class SutServiceTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Internal implementation was exposed
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Internal implementation was exposed
    ...
  }
}

class SutService {
  private FooService fooService;

  public SutService(FooService fooService) {
    this.fooService = fooService;
  }

  public void someMethod() {
    fooService.bar();
  }
}

interface FooService{
  int bar();
}

At this time, if the method name or parameter of the DOC changes, in other words, if the method signature changes, the test code must also be modified.

class RefactoringTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Break
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Break
    ...
  }
}

interface FooService{
  Bar bar(Factor factor);
}

In the case of the Test double created manually, Since stubbing is performed outside the test, redundancy is reduced, so refactoring resistance is little less. However, if you use the Mock library, you suffer from having to modify all the tests because you stub inside the test.

  • The credibility is low.

When you write stubbing, you write it expecting the DOC to actually work in production like that. However, the real object may not work as expected.

  • The amount of code can be too much.

The amount of code can be too much for the reason mentioned at the end in "It has low resistance to refactoring."

Conclusion

Although each test double was summarized as simply as possible to make it easy to distinguish, it may not be clearly distinguished when working on the actual project. In fact, this is not unusual, and MSDN Magazine uses the following figure as an example and states that, unlike theory, the difference between each test double is actually ambiguous.

I think I can't do anything else if I focus on terms, so I'm going to focus on the role of the object.

Reference

  • Mocks Aren't Stubs
  • Exploring The Continuum Of Test Doubles
  • What's the difference between a mock & stub?
  • Stub: Mocking Object For Tests In Yuk Recycle
  • xUnit Patterns
  • Test Double - Korean
  • Mock object abuse ruins the test code. - Korean

item.title: Spy, Stub, Fake 그리고 Mock에 대해서 알아보자.
item.description: Mockito에 대해서 정리하기 위해 자료를 검색하던 중, 모 블로거의 test double에 관한 글에서 가짜 객체를 Stub이라 부르는 것을 발견했다. 가짜 객체라곤 Mock 밖에 몰라서 전부 Mock이라고 생각하고 있었는데, 왜 Stub이라는 단어를 사용했는...
item.content:encoded:
Mockito에 대해서 정리하기 위해 자료를 검색하던 중, 모 블로거의 test double에 관한 글에서 가짜 객체를 Stub이라 부르는 것을 발견했다. 가짜 객체라곤 Mock 밖에 몰라서 전부 Mock이라고 생각하고 있었는데, 왜 Stub이라는 단어를 사용했는지 궁금해져서 조사해봤다.

Test double

Test double(테스트 대역)은 영화 속 배우를 대신하는 Stunt double(스턴트 대역)에서 유래되었으며, 테스트를 진행하기 어려운 경우 이를 대신해 진행해 주는 객체를 뜻한다. 다른 방식으로 설명하자면 테스트 대상에 집중할 수 있도록, 의존하는 구성요소를 테스트에 용이하도록 단순화한 객체를 뜻한다.

여기서 테스트 대상을 System Under Test 줄여서 SUT라고 부르고, SUT가 의존하는 구성요소를 Depended-On Component 줄여서 DOC라고 부른다.

SUT와 DOC 간의 협력이 어색하지 않다면, 해당 기능(행동)을 하나의 단위로 보고 함께 테스트 하는 것을 Sociable test라 한다. Sociable test의 경우, 현재 시간과 같이 SUT가 제어할 수 없는 상황이나 Email처럼 비용이 많이 드는 코드를 테스트 하기 어렵다.

이와 반대로, SUT와 DOC의 의존성을 끊고 하나의 모듈 단위로 테스트하는 것을 Solitary test라 한다. Solitary test의 경우 DOC를 단순하고 통제할 수 있는 객체로 만들어 SUT가 외부 어떠한 요인에도 영향을 받지 않도록 고립시켜 테스트 할 수 있다. 이때 만드는 DOC가 바로 테스트 대역이다.

💡 테스트 대역의 필요성에 대해서 서술하다보니 sociable test가 부정적으로 표현됐는데, 실제로는 그렇지 않다. 추후에 Solitary test와 Sociable test의 장단점을 정리해 보도록 하겠다.

언제 사용하는가?

  • 제어할 수 없는 상황을 통제할 때.

랜덤 숫자, 현재 시간, DB에서 특정 레코드 읽기는 항상 같은 결과를 보장하지 않는다. 또한 네트워크 오류나 외부 서비스의 상태가 항상 정상이 아닐 수도 있다. 이처럼 SUT가 제어할 수 없는 상황을 일관된 상황으로 통제할 때 사용할 수 있다.

  • 비용이 많이 드는 테스트를 할 때.

이메일, SMS 또는 메신저 등으로 보내는 템플릿이나 메시지큐에 보내는 메시지 내용 등은 테스트 비용이 많이 들어 테스트하기 어려운 기능이다. 이 경우, 검증에 필요한 내용을 테스트 대역에 저장한 뒤 검증할 수 있다.

  • 테스트 작성을 위한 환경 구축할 때.

특정 기능이 아직 구현되지 않아 테스트를 할 수 없는 경우 사용할 수 있다.

Test double의 종류

Dummy

가장 기본적인 테스트 대역으로, 파라미터 값으로 넘길때만 사용되며 실제로 쓰이지는 않는 객체이다. 필드에 기본값만 채워져있는 객체나 메소드 구현부가 비어있는 객체가 이에 해당된다.

@Test
void dummyTest() {
    Article dummyArticle = new Article("", "", new Date(), 0);
    
    assertThrows(Exception.class, () -> articleService.register(dummyArticle));
}

class ConsolePrinter {
    public void print(String text){
        System.out.println(text);
    }
}

class DummyConsolePrinter extends ConsolePrinter {
    @Override
    public void print(String text) {}
}

Stub

Dummy에서 한 단계 업그레이드 된 객체이며 하드 코딩된 값을 반환한다. 테스트 중 발생하는 호출에 대해서 미리 결과를 준비해놓는 것은 Stubbing이라 한다.

class StubArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    @Override
    public List<Article> getAll() {
        return List.of(
                new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1),
                new Article("title_2", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 8, 23, 10, 50), 0)
        );
    }
}

Spy

실제 객체와 동일한 동작을 할 수도 있고 Stub으로써 동작할 수도 있다. 호출이 되었을 때, 테스트 검증에 필요한 데이터를 저장한다.

class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    void spyTest() {
        SpyArticleRepository spy = new SpyArticleRepository();
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spy);
        Article stub1 = new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1);
        Article stub2 = new Article("title_2", "content_2", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 6, 29, 11, 15), 2);

        Long id1 = service.register(stub1);
        Long id2 = service.register(stub2);

        assertEquals(1, id1);
        assertEquals(2, id2);
        assertEquals(2, spy.getArticles().size());      // Spy's save behavior happened 2 times

    }
}

class SpyArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    private List<Article> articles = new ArrayList<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        articles.add(article);
        return 1L;
    }

    public List<Article> getArticles() {
        return articles;
    }
}

class ArticleService {
    private ArticleRepository repository;

    public ArticleService(ArticleRepository repository) {
        this.repository = repository;
    }

    public Long register(Article article) {
        return repository.save(article);
    }
}

Fake

실제 객체의 동작을 단순화해서 구현한 객체이며, 동적으로 동작은 하지만 프로덕션 환경에서 사용하기는 적합하지 않다. HashMap을 이용해서 인메모리 DB를 구현한 것을 예로 들 수 있다.

class ArticleRepositoryFake implements ArticleRepository {

    private Map<Long, Article> articles = new HashMap<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        Long existId = article.getId();
        if (existId == null) {
            return createNewArticle(article);
        }
        updateOldArticle(existId, article);
        return existId;
    }

    private void updateOldArticle(Long existId, Article article) {
        articles.put(existId, article);
    }

    private Long createNewArticle(Article article) {
        Long id = (long) (getAll().size() + 1);
        this.articles.put(id, article);
        return id;
    }

}

Mock

Mock 라이브러리를 통해 동적으로 생성한 객체이다. 구성하기에 따라 Dummy, Stub 또는 Spy처럼 동작한다.

💡 Mock 라이브러리 또는 테스트 프레임워크를 이용해서 Spy 객체 생성하면, 보통은 실제 객체처럼 동작하며 원하는 메소드를 선택적으로 Stubbing 해서 사용할 수 있다. 필자가 관심을 두고 있는 Mockito, Mockk, Spock 그리고 Jest에서 확인했으나, 혹시 다른 경우가 있다면 제보 바란다.

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a dummy")
    void mockTest1(@Mock Member dummyMember) {
        Article article = new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, dummyMember);
        ArticleRepository repository = new ArticleRepository();

        assertDoesNotThrow(() -> repository.save(article));
    }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a stub")
    void mockTest2(@Mock ArticleRepository stubRepository) {
        when(stubRepository.findById(anyLong())).thenReturn(new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, new Member("","")));
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(stubRepository);

        Article article = service.fetch(1L);

        assertEquals("Title 1", article.getTitle());

    }

    @Spy
    ArticleRepository spyRepository;

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a spy")
    void mockTest3() {
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spyRepository);

        service.fetch(1L);
        service.fetch(2L);

        verify(spyRepository, times(2)).findById(anyLong());

    }
}

테스트 대역의 한계

  • 리팩토리 내성이 낮다.

리팩토리 내성이 낮다는 말이 와닿지 않을 수 있는데, 관리 비용이 증가한다는 것과 같은 말이다. 예제를 통해 확인해보자. 테스트 대역을 이용하면 아래처럼 SUT의 내부 구현이 외부에 드러나게 된다.

class SutServiceTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Internal implementation was exposed
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Internal implementation was exposed
    ...
  }
}

class SutService {
  private FooService fooService;

  public SutService(FooService fooService) {
    this.fooService = fooService;
  }

  public void someMethod() {
    fooService.bar();
  }
}

interface FooService{
  int bar();
}

이때 DOC의 메소드 이름이나 파라미터가 변경될 경우, 다시 말해 메소드 시그니처가 변경될 경우 테스트 코드도 같이 수정되어야 한다.

class RefactoringTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Break
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Break
    ...
  }
}

interface FooService{
  Bar bar(Factor factor);
}

직접 만드는 테스트 대역의 경우에는 테스트 외부에서 Stubbing을 하기 때문에 중복이 줄어 그 정도가 덜하다. 그러나 Mock 라이브러리를 이용하는 경우, 테스트 내부에서 Stubbing을 하기 때문에 모든 테스트를 수정해야 하는 고통을 겪게 된다.

  • 신뢰도가 떨어진다.

Stubbing을 작성할 때, production에서 DOC가 실제 그렇게 동작하길 기대하면서 작성한다. 하지만 막상 실제 객체는 예상한대로 동작하지 않을 수 있다.

  • 코드량이 지나치게 많아질 수 있다.

"리팩토리 내성이 낮다." 의 끝에서 언급한 이유로 코드량이 지나치게 많아질 수 있다.

결론

각 테스트 대역을 구분하기 쉽게 최대한 간단하게 정리했지만, 실제 프로젝트를 진행하다보면 구분히 명확히 되지 않을 수 있다. 이는 사실 그리 이상한 일이 아닌데, MSDN 메거진에서는 아래의 그림을 예시로 들며 이론과 달리 실제로는 각 테스트 대역의 차이점이 모호하다고 얘기한다.

용어보다는 객체의 역할에 집중하는 것이 더 생산적일 것 같다.

Reference

  • Mocks Aren't Stubs
  • Exploring The Continuum Of Test Doubles
  • What's the difference between a mock & stub?
  • Stub: Mocking Object For Tests In Yuk Recycle
  • xUnit Patterns
  • Test Double
  • Mock 객체 남용은 테스트 코드를 망친다.

item.title: Starting unit test with JUnit 5 Part 2
item.description: In this article, there is no problem in testing even if you don't know it, but if you know it, you will organize the information that helps you tes...
item.content:encoded:
In this article, there is no problem in testing even if you don't know it, but if you know it, you will organize the information that helps you test it easily.

@ParameterizedTest

It is used to repeat the test several times using different arguments. Used in conjunction with the source annotation that specifies the value of the argument to be repeated. There are multiple source annotations, but knowing @ValueSource, @CsvSource is big enough.

@ValueSource

Specifies a single array of literal values as an argument. The supported types are short, byte, char, int, long, float, double, boolean, String and Class

@ParameterizedTest
@ValueSource(ints = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5})
void parameterizedTestExample(int candidate) {
    assertTrue(candidate < 10);
}

@CsvSource

Specifies comma-separated values as arguments. Each argument represents a CSV record.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource({"ninja,700", "assassin,850", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })
void csvSourceExample(String name, int score) { }

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

💡 From here on, I will omit the unnecessary parts so that I can focus on the changed attribute values of @CsvSource.

The useHeadersInDisplayName property allows the first record to be optionally used as a CSV header. If set to true, the first record is recognized as a header, skipped, and displayed as Display name.

@CsvSource(
    value = {"NAME,SCORE", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F"},
    useHeadersInDisplayName = true
)

[1] NAME = happy time, SCORE = 1_100
[2] NAME = @user-01x0w, SCORE = 0xE3F

The default delimiter is a comma (','). Char type delimiter can be specified through the delimiter attribute, and String type delimiter can be specified through the delimiterString attribute. Using the two properties together results in PreconditionViolationException.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'happy time':1_100", "@user-01x0w:0xE3F" }, 
    delimiter = ':'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

Use the default quotation marks (') as quotation marks. An empty string enclosed in quotation marks returns an empty string unless you set a separate value for the emptyValue attribute.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "\"happy time\",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    quoteCharacter = '\"'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvSource({"'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })

[1] , 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    emptyValue = "{}"
)

[1] {}, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

The string specified in the nullValues property is replaced with null.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "NIL,1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" },
    nullValues = "NIL"
)

[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

If ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false is set, the front and back spaces are left intact.

💡 The name attribute will be described in the Customizing display name section.

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[" + INDEX_PLACEHOLDER + "] ''{0}'', {1}")
@CsvSource(
        value = { "' happy time ',1_100", "@user-01x0w ,0xE3F" },
        ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false
)

[1] ' happy time', 1_100
[2] '@user-01x0w ', 0xE3F

A column with no value returns null.

@CsvSource(
    value = { ",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
)

[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

In Java 15 and later, textBlock properties can be used to improve readability. All rows beginning with the # symbol are treated as annotations and must begin without leading spaces.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource(delimiter = '|', quoteCharacter = '"', textBlock = """
#-----------------------------
#     NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------
""")
void CsvSourceWithTextBlockExample(String nickName, int score) {
    //...
}

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvFileSource

Gets the CSV file and specifies it as an argument. The useHeadersInDisplayName, delimiter attribute is the same as @CsvSource, and the quoteCharacter attribute is different in that the default quotation marks are double quotes.

#-----------------------------
      NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromClasspathExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(files = "src/test/resources/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromFileExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[{index}] {arguments}")
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", useHeadersInDisplayName = true, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceAndHeadersExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

The output results are omitted because they have already been covered a lot.

Null and Empty Sources

  • @EmptySource : Specifies an empty string as an argument value.
  • @NullSource : Specifies null as the argument value.
  • @NullAndEmptySource : It is an annotation that combines the two above.
import org.junit.platform.commons.util.StringUtils;

@ParameterizedTest
@NullSource
@EmptySource
@ValueSource(strings = { " ", "   ", "\t", "\n" })
void nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String text) {
    assertTrue(StringUtils.isBlank(text));
}

[1] null    nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[1] 0.012s  passed
[2]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[2] 0s      passed
[3]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[3] 0s      passed
[4]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[4] 0s      passed
[5]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[5] 0s      passed
[6]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[6] 0.001s  passed

@EnumSource

Specifies the Enum constant as an argument. For the value attribute, you must enter an enum class. This value is optional.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(ChronoUnit.class)
void enumSourceExample(TemporalUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource
void enumSourceWithoutValueExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);
}

💡 If you omit the value attribute, you will not receive automatic completion support for the names attribute, so please keep this in mind. Checked in Intelli J 2023.1.2.

In the names attribute, enter the name of the enum constant as a string. This value is optional, in which case all constants are applied.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(value = ChronoUnit.class, names = { "FOREVER", "ERAS" })
void enumSourceIncludeExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertTrue(EnumSet.of(ChronoUnit.FOREVER, ChronoUnit.ERAS).contains(unit));
}

The mode attribute specifies the mode for which constant to select in the enum class. INCLUDE, EXCLUDE, MATCH_ALL and MATCH_ANY can be used.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(mode = Mode.EXCLUDE, names = { "CENTURIES", "DECADES" })
void EnumSourceExcludeExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertFalse(EnumSet.of(ChronoUnit.CENTURIES, ChronoUnit.DECADES).contains(unit));
}

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(mode = Mode.MATCH_ALL, names = "^.*DAYS$")
void enumSourceRegexExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertTrue(unit.name().endsWith("DAYS"));
}

@MethodSource

Specify arguments through one or more factory methods. The factory method within the test class should be a static method unless the life cycle of the test class is PER_CLASS, and the factory method of the external class should always be a static method.

The factory method should return Stream<T>. If a single argument is required, just return the Stream of the method's argument type.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("fruitProvider")
void explicitLocalMethodSourceExample(String parameter) {
    assertNotNull(parameter);
}

static Stream<String> fruitProvider() {
    return Stream.of("cherry", "durian");
}

It also supports stream for primitive types such as DoubleStream, IntStream, and LongStream.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("intRange")
void intStreamMethodSourceExample(int parameter) {
    assertNotEquals(9, parameter);
}

static IntStream intRange() {
    return IntStream.rangeClosed(1, 20).skip(10);
}

If multiple arguments are required, return Stream<Arguments> of type arguments(Object...).

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("workingSchedule")
void multiParamsMethodSourceExample(String name, int workingHours, Set<DayOfWeek> dayOfWeeks) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertTrue(workingHours >= 1 && workingHours <= 10);
    assertEquals(2, dayOfWeeks.size());
}

static Stream<Arguments> workingSchedule() {
    return Stream.of(
            arguments("John", 3, EnumSet.of(DayOfWeek.MONDAY, DayOfWeek.WEDNESDAY)),
            arguments("Sam", 9, EnumSet.of(DayOfWeek.TUESDAY, DayOfWeek.THURSDAY))
    );
}

Refer to the example below when referring to the external factory method.

.
└── src
    ├── main
    │   └── ...
    └── test
        ├── java
        │   ├── ...
        │   ├── xyz
        │   │   └── besfir
        │   │       └── StringsProviders.java
        │   └── ExternalMethodSourceDemo.java
        └── resources


import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.MethodSource;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertNotNull;

class ExternalMethodSourceDemo {

    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders#theSkyIsBlue")
    void externalMethodSourceExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders$NestingProviders#theFruits")
    void externalMethodSourceWithNestingClassExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
}

package xyz.besfir;

import java.util.stream.Stream;

class StringsProviders {

    static Stream<String> theSkyIsBlue() {
        return Stream.of("The", "sky", "is", "blue");
    }

    class NestingProviders {

        static Stream<String> theFruits() {
            return Stream.of("apple", "banana", "cherry", "durian");
        }
    }
}

If the factory method name and the @ParameterizedTest annotated method name are the same, it can be omitted.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource
void defaultLocalMethodSourceExample(String argument) {
    assertNotNull(argument);
}

static Stream<String> defaultLocalMethodSourceExample() {
    return Stream.of("apple", "banana");
}

@ArgumentsSource

Used to specify a custom ArgumentsProvider. The source annotations summarized above are internally implementing functions using @ArgumentsSource and ArgumentsProvider.

@Target({ ElementType.ANNOTATION_TYPE, ElementType.METHOD })
@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Documented
@API(status = STABLE, since = "5.7")
@ArgumentsSource(ValueArgumentsProvider.class)
public @interface ValueSource {
  ...
}

You can use it if you want to implement it additionally in addition to the source annotation provided.

@RepeatedTest

Repeat the test as many times as you specify.

@RepeatedTest(10)
void equalsTest() {
    assertEquals(1 + 1, 2);
}

@Nested

Used to group tests, only for non-static nested classes. Before Java 16, @BeforeAll and @AfterAll cannot be used because static methods cannot be declared on nested classes. However, it can be used if the test instance life cycle is PER_CLASS. After Java 16, this is not the case because static methods can be declared on nested classes.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

class NestedExampleTest {

    @Nested
    class SomeGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_1 {
        
            @Test
            void test1() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_2 {
        
            @Test
            void test2() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }


    @Nested
    class OtherGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTestA {

            @Test
            void testA() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTestB {
        
            @Test
            void testB() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }
}

@Timeout

An annotation that causes an exception to occur and terminate immediately if the method exceeds the specified duration, and can be annotated to a test, test factory, test template, or life cycle method. It can also be annotated to class level and applied in common to multiple methods, and the nested classes annotated @Nested are also affected. However, if set at the class level, it does not apply to the life cycle method.

In terms of description alone, the use seems to be the same as that of assertTimeoutPreemptively(), but @Timeout uses it to replace it in certain scenarios. Because assertTimeoutPreemptively() runs on a different thread than the test method, so spring transaction does not work as desired. On the other hand, @Timeout is basically used in the same thread as the test method, so it is used as an alternative if mentioned above. Thread behavior can be changed through threadMode properties.

  • INFERRED : Default value, and it follows the setting parameter or the setting file's junit.jupiter.execution.timeout.thread.mode.default property value. If not, operate as SAME_THREAD.
  • SAME_THREAD : Runs on the same thread as the test method.
  • SEPARATE_THREAD : It runs on a different thread than the test method.

unit attribute allows you to select a unit of time, and the basic unit is SECONDS.

@Test
@Timeout(value = 500, unit = TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
void timeoutAnnotationExample() throws InterruptedException {
    Thread.sleep(10_000);
}

@TempDir

Used to create and cleanup temporary directories. You can annotate to a parameter or field-level Path or File type.

@Test
void tempDirExample(@TempDir Path source, @TempDir Path target) throws IOException {
    Path sourceFile = source.resolve("test.txt");
    Files.write(sourceFile, "a,b,c".getBytes());

    Path targetFile = Files.copy(sourceFile, target.resolve("test.txt"));

    assertNotEquals(sourceFile, targetFile);
    assertEquals(singletonList("a,b,c"), Files.readAllLines(targetFile));
}

cleanup attribute allow you to specify cleanup actions, and there are four modes: DEFAULT, ALWAYS, ON_SUCCESS and NEVERDEFAULT mode follows the setting parameter or the setting file's junit.jupiter.tempdir.cleanup.mode.default property value, and operates as ALWAYS mode if there is no value.

Extension Model

Declarative Extension Registration

You can register one or more extensions using @ExtendWith.

@ExtendWith({ DatabaseExtension.class, WebServerExtension.class})
class ExtensionModelTest { }
// or 
@ExtendWith(DatabaseExtension.class)
@ExtendWith(WebServerExtension.class)
class ExtensionModelTest { }

Programmatic Extension Registration

@RegisterExtension is used when you need to put some value into the extension from the outside. The extended field should not be a private field or null.

If the @RegisterExtension field is static, it is registered later than the extensions registered in @ExtendWith. In this case, both the class-level callback and the method-level callback of the extension are invoked.

public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    static LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type static version In beforeAll : RegisterExtensionTest
Type static version In beforeEach : someTest()

If the @RegisterExtension field is non-static, it is registered later than the extensions specified in @ExtendWith, and only invokes the method-level callback of the extension.

public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("non-static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type non-static version In beforeEach : someTest()

However, if the life cycle of the test instance is PER_CLASS, it is registered before the extensions specified in @ExtendWith, and both class-level and method-level callbacks of the extension are invoked.

@TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS)
public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type non-static version In beforeAll : RegisterExtensionTest
Type non-static version In beforeEach : someTest()

public class LoggingExtension implements BeforeAllCallback, BeforeEachCallback {

    private final String type;

    public LoggingExtension(String type) {
        this.type = type;
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeAll(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeAll : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeEach(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeEach : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    public String getType() {
        return type;
    }
}

JUnit Platform configuration file

The file must be in the src/test/resources/junit-platform.properties path and can be configured for JUnit global settings.

junit.jupiter.execution.timeout.thread.mode.default = same_thread
junit.jupiter.tempdir.cleanup.mode.default = always
...

Customizing display name

Repeatable tests, such as @RepeatedTest and @ParameterizedTest, can customize the invocation display name with the name attribute to identify each test. In this case, since the name attribute is a message format pattern, it must be used twice to display one small quotation mark. The supported placeholders are as follows.

PlaceholderDescription
{displayName}the display name of the method
{index}the current invocation index (1-based)
{arguments}the complete, comma-separated arguments list
{argumentsWithNames}the complete, comma-separated arguments list with parameter names
{0}, {1}, ...an individual argument

Example

@DisplayName("Display name of container")
@ParameterizedTest(name = "{index} ==> the rank of ''{0}'' is {1}")
@CsvSource({ "apple, 1", "banana, 2", "'lemon, lime', 3" })
void customDisplayNamesExample(String fruit, int rank) { }

Reference

  • JUnit user guide
  • JUnit5 Programmatic Extension Registration with @RegisterExtension

item.title: JUnit 5로 단위 테스트 시작하기 2편
item.description: 이번 글에서는 모르더라도 테스트를 하는데 지장은 없지만, 알면 쉽게 테스트 하는데 도움이 되는 정보들을 알아볼 예정이다.
item.content:encoded:
이번 글에서는 모르더라도 테스트하는 데 지장은 없지만, 알면 테스트를 쉽게 하는 데 도움을 주는 정보들을 정리할 예정이다.

@ParameterizedTest

서로 다른 인수를 이용해서 테스트를 여러번 반복하기 위해 사용한다. 반복될 인수 값을 지정하는 소스 어노테이션과 함께 사용한다. 여러 소스 어노테이션이 있지만 @ValueSource, @CsvSource만 알아도 충분히 큰 도움이 된다.

@ValueSource

리터럴 값의 단일 배열을 인수로 지정한다. 지원하는 타입은 short, byte, char, int, long, float, double, boolean, String, Class이다.

@ParameterizedTest
@ValueSource(ints = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5})
void parameterizedTestExample(int candidate) {
    assertTrue(candidate < 10);
}

@CsvSource

쉼표로 구분된 값(Comma Separated Value)을 인수로 지정한다. 각 인수는 CSV 레코드를 나타낸다.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource({"ninja,700", "assassin,850", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })
void csvSourceExample(String name, int score) { }

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

💡 여기서부터 @CsvSource의 바뀐 속성값에 집중할 수 있도록 필요없는 부분은 생략하겠다.

useHeadersInDisplayName 속성을 이용해서 첫번째 레코드를 선택적으로 CSV 헤더로 사용할 수 있다. true로 설정하면 첫번째 레코드를 헤더로 인식해서 스킵하고 Display name으로 표시한다.

@CsvSource(
    value = {"NAME,SCORE", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F"},
    useHeadersInDisplayName = true
)

[1] NAME = happy time, SCORE = 1_100
[2] NAME = @user-01x0w, SCORE = 0xE3F

기본 구분자는 콤마(,)이다. delimiter 속성을 통해서 Char 타입 구분자를 지정할 수 있으며, delimiterString 속성을 통해서 String 타입 구분자를 지정할 수 있다. 두 속성을 함께 사용하면 PreconditionViolationException이 발생한다.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'happy time':1_100", "@user-01x0w:0xE3F" }, 
    delimiter = ':'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

따옴표 문자로 작은 따옴표(')를 기본으로 사용한다. 따옴표 문자로 묶인 빈문자열은 emptyValue 속성에 별도 값을 설정하지 않는다면 빈문자열을 반환한다.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "\"happy time\",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    quoteCharacter = '\"'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvSource({"'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })

[1] , 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    emptyValue = "{}"
)

[1] {}, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

nullValues 속성에 지정한 문자열은 null로 치환된다.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "NIL,1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" },
    nullValues = "NIL"
)

[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false 설정을 하면 앞뒤 공백을 그대로 둔다.

💡 name 속성은 Display name 커스터마이징 하기 단원에서 설명한다.

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[" + INDEX_PLACEHOLDER + "] ''{0}'', {1}")
@CsvSource(
        value = { "' happy time ',1_100", "@user-01x0w ,0xE3F" },
        ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false
)

[1] ' happy time', 1_100
[2] '@user-01x0w ', 0xE3F

값이 없는 컬럼은 null을 반환한다.

@CsvSource(
    value = { ",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
)

[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

Java 15 이상에서는 textBlock 속성을 사용해서 가독성을 향상시킬 수 있다. #기호로 시작하는 모든 행은 주석 처리되며, 선행 공백없이 시작되야 한다.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource(delimiter = '|', quoteCharacter = '"', textBlock = """
#-----------------------------
#     NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------
""")
void CsvSourceWithTextBlockExample(String nickName, int score) {
    //...
}

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvFileSource

CSV 파일을 가져와서 인수로 지정한다. useHeadersInDisplayName, delimiter 속성은 @CsvSource과 같으며, quoteCharacter 속성은 기본 따옴표가 큰 따옴표인 점이 다르다.

#-----------------------------
      NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromClasspathExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(files = "src/test/resources/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromFileExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[{index}] {arguments}")
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", useHeadersInDisplayName = true, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceAndHeadersExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

출력 결과는 이미 많이 다뤘으므로 생략한다.

Null and Empty Sources

  • @EmptySource: 인수 값으로 빈문자열을 지정한다.
  • @NullSource: 인수 값으로 null을 지정한다.
  • @NullAndEmptySource : 위의 2개를 합친 어노테이션이다.
